activities_projects.title: Religions Around Us
activities_projects.objective: Understand the diversity of religions in society
steps: List different religions you know (Hinduism, Islam, Christianity, Sikhism, etc.)
Write one festival celebrated in each religion
Share your list with classmates
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct examples of religions and festivals (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Festival Chart
activities_projects.objective: Learn about festivals celebrated by different religions
steps: Choose 5 festivals from different religions
Write the religion and the importance of the festival
Decorate and present the chart

activities_projects.title: Meaning of Secularism
steps: Write the meaning of secularism in your own words
Write why secularism is important in India
Give one example of secular practice
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the basic concept of secularism

activities_projects.title: Equality for All Religions
activities_projects.objective: Learn that the state treats all religions equally
steps: Write two examples of how the government treats all religions equally
Discuss why equality is important
Write a short paragraph about it

activities_projects.title: Places of Worship List
steps: List places of worship like temple, mosque, church, gurudwara
Write which religion each place belongs to
Write one thing people do there

activities_projects.title: Respect for All Religions
steps: Write three ways we can respect other religions
Give an example for each
Share your ideas in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops values of respect and tolerance

activities_projects.title: Government and Religion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of government in a secular state
steps: Write two responsibilities of the government in a secular country
Explain why the government should not favour any religion
Write a short answer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the role of government in secularism

activities_projects.title: Words of Secularism
activities_projects.objective: Learn key vocabulary from the chapter
steps: Write words like secularism, religion, equality, discrimination
Write simple meanings for each word
Use one word in a sentence

activities_projects.title: Short Story on Unity
steps: Write a short story where people from different religions help each other
Mention the problem they solved together
Explain the message of the story

activities_projects.title: Class Discussion on Secularism
steps: Discuss with classmates why secularism is important in India
List two points from the discussion
Write a short summary
